rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR PARTNERS, A NEW YORK PARTNERSHIP VS. GUAN SHAO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Order To Show Cause, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 13, 2024 line 1. ASSIGNEE NDS, LLC. Notice Of Hearing On Order To Show Cause, Etc. is continued to February 28, 2024 for the moving party to file a supplemental brief addressing: (1) whether the moving party is seeking an issuance of an Order to Show Cause Re: Sale of Dwelling to be set for a later date or is seeking an order authorizing the sale of dwelling at this hearing; (2) to provide a filed endorsed copy of the previously entered Order to Show cause which appears to be referenced in the Notice of Hearing filed on November 11, 2023: "Notice is hereby given that the above entitled Court has she this matter of an order to show cause.."; and (3) to explain the basis for the sale of this property if the property does not belong to the judgment debtor. See Application for Sale of Dwelling paragraph 6: " the Judgment Debtor does not appear to be the owner of the Property" Supplemental brief shall not exceed 2 pages, excluding exhibits and shall be limited to the issues listed above. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
